extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint after Jayco-AlUla perfectly timed the catch of an eight-rider breakaway on the final 14.6km circuit lap.
tactical_patterns: VAN AERT Wout doing water-carrying duties suggests Jumbo-Visma already shifting seasonal focus away from supporting sprint trains.
BALLERINI Davide substituting for JAKOBSEN Fabio reveals Soudal Quick-Step's sprint leadership flexibility even within single-stage decisions.
Eight-rider breakaway quality forced peloton to time catch for final circuit lap, narrower margin than sprinters' teams typically prefer.
Pinch points and chicane at 750m marker pre-select top-10 positioning, making lead-out quality matter less than usual positioning discipline.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: doubled stage wins and showed superior positioning judgment through technical 750m chicane finale
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: reached podium without dedicated lead-out train, suggesting individual sprint craft remains elite-level
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: positive: contended finale while normally on lead-out duty for MAYRHOFER Marius, signals sprint ambition emerging
rider_signals.BALLERINI Davide: neutral: substituted as Quick-Step sprinter over JAKOBSEN Fabio, unclear if tactical choice or form-based decision
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: neutral: relegated to water-carrying domestique role suggests Jumbo prioritizing ROGLIČ Primož GC over any sprint investment
